ferx 0.1.5
  • Reference
  • Articles
  • Changelog

    ferx: Nonlinear Mixed Effects Modeling with a Rust Backend

    ferx-package.Rd

    ferx provides a user-facing R API for population PK / NLME modeling that delegates to a high-performance Rust engine (ferx-core) with Enzyme automatic differentiation for exact gradients. Models are written in a compact .ferx DSL (parameters, individual_parameters, structural_model, odes, error_model, initial_values, fit_options, optional scaling).

    Where to start

    GoalFunction
    Get a worked exampleferx_example
    Inspect dataset columnsferx_columns
    Create a model from a templateferx_model_new
    Inspect / edit an existing modelferx_model_show, ferx_model_edit, ferx_model_validate, ferx_model_inspect
    Sanity-check starting valuesferx_check_init, ferx_inits_from_nca
    Fit a model (FOCE / FOCEI / GN / SAEM / IMP)ferx_fit
    Inspect a fitsummary.ferx_fit, ferx_estimates, ferx_cor_matrix, check_diagnostics
    Optimizer traceferx_trace, ferx_plot_trace
    Covariate screen on EBE ETAsferx_eta_cov
    Uncertainty (SIR)ferx_sir
    Population / individual predictionsferx_predict
    Simulate (with or without parameter uncertainty)ferx_simulate, ferx_simulate_with_uncertainty
    Save / restore a fitferx_save_fit, ferx_load_fit

    Functions are grouped on the reference index under fitting, simulation, model-editing, diagnostics, and utilities; each function's help page links to its siblings via See Also.

    Build modes

    ferx works with or without the Enzyme autodiff toolchain. Without Enzyme, gradients fall back to finite differences; fits remain correct but are slower and gradient = "ad" is unavailable. See ferx_rust_autodiff_enabled() (internal) and the startup message for the active mode.

    See also

    Useful links:

    • https://ferx-nlme.github.io/reference/
